text: The patient is positioned laterally, and three robotic ports along with one assistant port are placed. The surgeon drops the colon medially to expose the ureter, which is identified and carefully freed from surrounding fibrous tissue. Multiple biopsies are taken from the perirectal and retroperitoneal tissues. The descending colon is interposed between the ureter and the posterior abdominal wall, and sutured to the lateral abdominal wall to maintain its position. An abdominal drain is placed to monitor for potential urine leaks.
